one_time_description: First Nations Drinking Water Settlement payment received as part of court-approved settlement for drinking water advisories between 1995 and 2021
economic_notes: Ontario First Nation Limited Partnership distributions of $595,153. Greenstone Gold Mine execution payment distributed $1,000 per capita to members in 2020. Additional drinking water settlement payments expected to begin 2024 based on individual community member claims. Investments in Landmark Inn LP, Ne-Daa-Kii-Me-Naan Inc., Agoke Development LP, Ginoogam Development Corporation, Kenogamisis Energy LP, Minodahmun Development LP.
notes: Audit is qualified on three bases: (1) inability to verify investments in various entities, (2) no consolidated budgets prepared so budgeted information not presented, and (3) PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations not yet assessed. Five consolidated subsidiaries all listed as inactive. Honoraria of $255,000 recorded across segments likely representing Chief and Council remuneration but no formal schedule of remuneration included. $500,000 drinking water settlement received is a material one-time item. CMHC replacement reserve of $148,024 is the only identified restricted cash. Obligation under capital lease of $51,312 exists in addition to long-term debt. 15 housing units identified as security for CMHC loans (7 + 8 units).
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
